1	import { Schema } from "effect";
2	import { TrimmedNonEmptyString } from "./baseSchemas";
3	import { ProviderModelOptions } from "./model";
4	import {
5	  ApprovalRequestId,
6	  EventId,
7	  IsoDateTime,
8	  ProviderItemId,
9	  ThreadId,
10	  TurnId,
11	} from "./baseSchemas";
12	import {
13	  ChatAttachment,
14	  PROVIDER_SEND_TURN_MAX_ATTACHMENTS,
15	  PROVIDER_SEND_TURN_MAX_INPUT_CHARS,
16	  ProviderApprovalDecision,
17	  ProviderApprovalPolicy,
18	  ProviderInteractionMode,
19	  ProviderKind,
20	  ProviderRequestKind,
21	  ProviderSandboxMode,
22	  ProviderServiceTier,
23	  ProviderUserInputAnswers,
24	  RuntimeMode,
25	} from "./orchestration";
26	
27	const TrimmedNonEmptyStringSchema = TrimmedNonEmptyString;
28	const ProviderSessionStatus = Schema.Literals([
29	  "connecting",
30	  "ready",
31	  "running",
32	  "error",
33	  "closed",
34	]);
35	
36	export const ProviderSession = Schema.Struct({
37	  provider: ProviderKind,
38	  status: ProviderSessionStatus,
39	  runtimeMode: RuntimeMode,
40	  cwd: Schema.optional(TrimmedNonEmptyStringSchema),
41	  model: Schema.optional(TrimmedNonEmptyStringSchema),
42	  threadId: ThreadId,
43	  resumeCursor: Schema.optional(Schema.Unknown),
44	  activeTurnId: Schema.optional(TurnId),
45	  createdAt: IsoDateTime,
46	  updatedAt: IsoDateTime,
47	  lastError: Schema.optional(TrimmedNonEmptyStringSchema),
48	});
49	export type ProviderSession = typeof ProviderSession.Type;
50	
51	const CodexProviderStartOptions = Schema.Struct({
52	  binaryPath: Schema.optional(TrimmedNonEmptyStringSchema),
53	  homePath: Schema.optional(TrimmedNonEmptyStringSchema),
54	});
55	
56	export const ProviderStartOptions = Schema.Struct({
57	  codex: Schema.optional(CodexProviderStartOptions),
58	});
59	export type ProviderStartOptions = typeof ProviderStartOptions.Type;
60	
61	export const ProviderSessionStartInput = Schema.Struct({
62	  threadId: ThreadId,
63	  provider: Schema.optional(ProviderKind),
64	  cwd: Schema.optional(TrimmedNonEmptyStringSchema),
65	  model: Schema.optional(TrimmedNonEmptyStringSchema),
66	  modelOptions: Schema.optional(ProviderModelOptions),
67	  resumeCursor: Schema.optional(Schema.Unknown),
68	  serviceTier: Schema.optional(Schema.NullOr(ProviderServiceTier)),
69	  approvalPolicy: Schema.optional(ProviderApprovalPolicy),
70	  sandboxMode: Schema.optional(ProviderSandboxMode),
71	  providerOptions: Schema.optional(ProviderStartOptions),
72	  runtimeMode: RuntimeMode,
73	});
74	export type ProviderSessionStartInput = typeof ProviderSessionStartInput.Type;
75	
76	export const ProviderSendTurnInput = Schema.Struct({
77	  threadId: ThreadId,
78	  input: Schema.optional(
79	    TrimmedNonEmptyStringSchema.check(Schema.isMaxLength(PROVIDER_SEND_TURN_MAX_INPUT_CHARS)),
80	  ),
81	  attachments: Schema.optional(
82	    Schema.Array(ChatAttachment).check(Schema.isMaxLength(PROVIDER_SEND_TURN_MAX_ATTACHMENTS)),
83	  ),
84	  model: Schema.optional(TrimmedNonEmptyStringSchema),
85	  serviceTier: Schema.optional(Schema.NullOr(ProviderServiceTier)),
86	  modelOptions: Schema.optional(ProviderModelOptions),
87	  interactionMode: Schema.optional(ProviderInteractionMode),
88	});
89	export type ProviderSendTurnInput = typeof ProviderSendTurnInput.Type;
90	
91	export const ProviderTurnStartResult = Schema.Struct({
92	  threadId: ThreadId,
93	  turnId: TurnId,
94	  resumeCursor: Schema.optional(Schema.Unknown),
95	});
96	export type ProviderTurnStartResult = typeof ProviderTurnStartResult.Type;
97	
98	export const ProviderInterruptTurnInput = Schema.Struct({
99	  threadId: ThreadId,
100	  turnId: Schema.optional(TurnId),
101	});

80	// -- Tagged Union of all request body schemas ─────────────────────────
81	
82	const tagRequestBody = <const Tag extends string, const Fields extends Schema.Struct.Fields>(
83	  tag: Tag,
84	  schema: Schema.Struct<Fields>,
85	) =>
86	  schema.mapFields(
87	    Struct.assign({ _tag: Schema.tag(tag) }),
88	    // PreserveChecks is safe here. No existing schema should have checks depending on the tag
89	    { unsafePreserveChecks: true },
90	  );
91	
92	const WebSocketRequestBody = Schema.Union([
93	  // Orchestration methods
94	  tagRequestBody(
95	    ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.dispatchCommand,
96	    Schema.Struct({ command: ClientOrchestrationCommand }),
97	  ),
98	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getSnapshot, OrchestrationGetSnapshotInput),
99	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getTurnDiff, OrchestrationGetTurnDiffInput),
100	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getFullThreadDiff, OrchestrationGetFullThreadDiffInput),
101	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.replayEvents, OrchestrationReplayEventsInput),
102	
103	  // Project Search
104	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsSearchEntries, ProjectSearchEntriesInput),
105	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsWriteFile, ProjectWriteFileInput),
106	
107	  // Shell methods
108	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.shellOpenInEditor, OpenInEditorInput),
109	
110	  // Git methods
111	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitPull, GitPullInput),
112	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitStatus, GitStatusInput),
113	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRunStackedAction, GitRunStackedActionInput),
114	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitListBranches, GitListBranchesInput),
115	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateWorktree, GitCreateWorktreeInput),
116	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRemoveWorktree, GitRemoveWorktreeInput),
117	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateBranch, GitCreateBranchInput),
118	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCheckout, GitCheckoutInput),
119	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitInit, GitInitInput),
120	
121	  // Terminal methods
122	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalOpen, TerminalOpenInput),
123	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalWrite, TerminalWriteInput),
124	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalResize, TerminalResizeInput),
125	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClear, TerminalClearInput),
126	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalRestart, TerminalRestartInput),
127	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClose, TerminalCloseInput),
128	
129	  // Server meta
130	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverGetConfig, Schema.Struct({})),
131	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverUpsertKeybinding, KeybindingRule),
132	]);

134	export const WebSocketRequest = Schema.Struct({

137	});
138	export type WebSocketRequest = typeof WebSocketRequest.Type;
139	
140	export const WebSocketResponse = Schema.Struct({
141	  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
142	  result: Schema.optional(Schema.Unknown),
143	  error: Schema.optional(
144	    Schema.Struct({
145	      message: Schema.String,
146	    }),
147	  ),
148	});
149	export type WebSocketResponse = typeof WebSocketResponse.Type;
150	
151	export const WsPush = Schema.Struct({
152	  type: Schema.Literal("push"),
153	  channel: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
154	  data: Schema.Unknown,
155	});
156	export type WsPush = typeof WsPush.Type;
157	
158	// ── Union of all server → client messages ─────────────────────────────
159	
160	export const WsResponse = Schema.Union([WebSocketResponse, WsPush]);
161	export type WsResponse = typeof WsResponse.Type;

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed report:

## Comprehensive Monorepo Exploration Report

### Overview
This is a TypeScript monorepo (`@t3tools/monorepo`) using Bun as the package manager with Turbo for build orchestration. The repo uses Effect.js for typed functional programming, Schema for validation, and Vitest for testing.

---

### ROOT CONFIGURATION

**Root Package.json:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/package.json`

- **Workspace setup:** Uses Yarn/Pnpm workspaces pattern with `apps/*`, `packages/*`, and `scripts` directories
- **Package manager:** Bun v1.3.9 (required)
- **Node version:** ^24.13.1
- **Key scripts:** dev, build, typecheck, test, lint (oxlint), format (oxfmt)
- **Catalog dependencies:** Uses centralized version management for common libraries:
  - `effect` (from PR feed)
  - `@effect/platform-node`, `@effect/sql-sqlite-bun`, `@effect/vitest` (from PR feed)
  - `TypeScript: ^5.7.3`
  - `Vitest: ^4.0.0`
  - `tsdown: ^0.20.3`
  - `@types/bun`, `@types/node`

**Base TypeScript Config:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/tsconfig.base.json`
- Target: ES2023
- Module: ESNext
- Module resolution: Bundler (modern)
- Strict mode enabled with strict compiler checks (`noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noImplicitOverride`)
- Synth defaults allowed for compatibility

---

### PACKAGES/SHARED - UTILITIES PACKAGE

**Package:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/packages/shared/`

**Package.json:**
- Name: `@t3tools/shared`
- Version: `0.0.0-alpha.1`
- Private: true
- **Exports:** Subpath exports for modular imports:
  - `./model` → `src/model.ts`
  - `./git` → `src/git.ts`
  - `./logging` → `src/logging.ts`
  - `./shell` → `src/shell.ts`
  - `./Net` → `src/Net.ts`
- **Dependencies:** `@t3tools/contracts` (workspace), `effect` (catalog)
- **Build:** TypeScript compilation with typecheck and vitest
- **Prepare:** Effect language service patching

**TSConfig:** Extends base config, includes only `src` directory

**Source Files:**

1. **model.ts** (79 lines)
   - Re-exports model constants from `@t3tools/contracts`
   - Functions: `getModelOptions()`, `getDefaultModel()`, `normalizeModelSlug()`, `resolveModelSlug()`, `resolveModelSlugForProvider()`, `getReasoningEffortOptions()`, `getDefaultReasoningEffort()`
   - Provides model resolution logic with aliases (e.g., "5.3" → "gpt-5.3-codex")
   - Sets use set structures for model slug validation

2. **git.ts** (62 lines)
   - Utilities for git branch name handling:
   - `sanitizeBranchFragment()` - Sanitizes strings to valid git branch fragments (lowercase, max 64 chars, strips special chars)
   - `sanitizeFeatureBranchName()` - Wraps with `feature/` prefix
   - `resolveAutoFeatureBranchName()` - Generates unique feature branch names with numeric suffixes when collisions occur

3. **logging.ts** (115 lines)
   - `RotatingFileSink` class for rotating log files
   - Constructor validates `maxBytes >= 1` and `maxFiles >= 1`
   - Methods: `write()`, `rotate()`, `pruneOverflowBackups()`, `readCurrentSize()`
   - Handles file I/O with optional error throwing
   - Rotates logs when size exceeded, maintains numbered backups (.1, .2, etc)

4. **shell.ts** (39 lines)
   - Functions for shell environment handling:
   - `extractPathFromShellOutput()` - Extracts PATH from shell output using markers
   - `readPathFromLoginShell()` - Executes login shell to read PATH environment variable with 5-second timeout
   - Exports `ExecFileSyncLike` type for dependency injection

5. **Net.ts** (181 lines) - Effect-based networking service
   - `NetError` - Tagged error class
   - `NetService` - Effect service with Layer
   - Methods:
     - `canListenOnHost(port, host)` - Check if TCP server can bind
     - `isPortAvailableOnLoopback(port)` - Check both IPv4 and IPv6 availability
     - `reserveLoopbackPort(host)` - Reserve ephemeral port and release immediately
     - `findAvailablePort(preferred)` - Prefer provided port or find available one
   - Handles `EADDRNOTAVAIL` as "available" for IPv6-absent hosts
   - Uses Effect callbacks for async operations

**Test Files:**
- `model.test.ts` - Tests model slug normalization, resolution, and defaults
- `shell.test.ts` - Tests shell PATH extraction
- `Net.test.ts` - Tests port availability and reservation logic

---

### PACKAGES/CONTRACTS - SCHEMA/TYPE DEFINITIONS

**Package:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/packages/contracts/`

**Package.json:**
- Name: `@t3tools/contracts`
- Version: `0.0.8`
- Private: true
- **Exports:** 
  - `.` (main entry) - Both CJS and ESM with tsdown
  - Types: `src/index.ts`
  - Import/require patterns for dual module support
- **Files:** Distributes `dist` directory
- **Build:** `tsdown` for dual-format bundling (esm, cjs) with .d.ts
- **Dependencies:** `effect` (catalog)
- **Prepare:** Effect language service patching

**TSConfig:** 
- Extends base config
- Adds `@effect/language-service` plugin with:
  - Diagnostic severity overrides (importFromBarrel: error)
  - `@effect/platform-node` namespace import packages

**Index Export:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/packages/contracts/src/index.ts`
- Barrel exports from 11 modules: baseSchemas, ipc, terminal, provider, providerRuntime, model, ws, keybindings, server, git, orchestration, editor, project

**Source Files:**

1. **baseSchemas.ts** (45 lines)
   - Base Effect schemas for common patterns:
   - `TrimmedString`, `TrimmedNonEmptyString`
   - `NonNegativeInt`, `PositiveInt`
   - `IsoDateTime` (branded string)
   - Entity ID types (branded schemas): ThreadId, ProjectId, CommandId, EventId, MessageId, TurnId, ProviderItemId, RuntimeSessionId, RuntimeItemId, RuntimeRequestId, RuntimeTaskId, ApprovalRequestId, CheckpointRef

2. **model.ts** (58 lines)
   - Model configuration constants:
   - `CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS`: ["xhigh", "high", "medium", "low"]
   - `MODEL_OPTIONS_BY_PROVIDER.codex`: 5 models (gpt-5.4, gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.3-codex-spark, gpt-5.2-codex, gpt-5.2)
   - `DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PROVIDER.codex`: "gpt-5.4"
   - `MODEL_SLUG_ALIASES_BY_PROVIDER`: Aliases for model shortcuts
   - Schema types for `CodexModelOptions`, `ProviderModelOptions`

3. **terminal.ts** (151 lines)
   - Comprehensive terminal session/event schemas
   - Input schemas: `TerminalOpenInput`, `TerminalWriteInput`, `TerminalResizeInput`, `TerminalClearInput`, `TerminalCloseInput`, `TerminalRestartInput`
   - Constraints: cols 20-400, rows 5-200, env 128 max properties, data max 65KB
   - Session snapshot: status (starting/running/exited/error), pid, history, exit code/signal
   - Event union: Started, Output, Exited, Error, Cleared, Restarted, Activity
   - All with threadId, terminalId, timestamps

4. **provider.ts** (141 lines)
   - Provider session and turn handling schemas:
   - Session status: connecting/ready/running/error/closed
   - `ProviderSession`: provider, status, runtimeMode, model, thread/turn IDs, created/updated timestamps
   - Input schemas: `ProviderSessionStartInput`, `ProviderSendTurnInput`, `ProviderInterruptTurnInput`, `ProviderStopSessionInput`, `ProviderRespondToRequestInput`, `ProviderRespondToUserInputInput`
   - `ProviderEvent`: structured logging for sessions/notifications/requests/errors with method, message, optional turnId/itemId/requestId

5. **providerRuntime.ts** (35,676 bytes - large file)
   - Complex runtime event handling from provider perspective
   - Event sources: codex.app-server.notification/request, codex.eventmsg, codex.sdk.thread-event
   - State literals: RuntimeSessionState, RuntimeThreadState, RuntimeTurnState, RuntimePlanStepStatus, RuntimeItemStatus, RuntimeContentStreamKind, RuntimeSessionExitKind, RuntimeErrorClass
   - Canonical item types: user_message, assistant_message, reasoning, plan, command_execution, file_change, mcp_tool_call, dynamic_tool_call, collab_agent_tool_call, web_search, image_view, review_entered/exited, context_compaction, error, unknown
   - Canonical request types: command_execution_approval, file_read_approval, file_change_approval, apply_patch_approval, exec_command_approval, tool_user_input, dynamic_tool_call, auth_tokens_refresh, unknown
   - Event type unions with 48+ event types (session.*, thread.*, turn.*, item.*, content.*, request.*, task.*, hook.*, tool.*, auth.*, account.*, mcp.*, model.*, config.*, deprecation.*, files.*, runtime.*)

6. **orchestration.ts** (500+ lines)
   - Main data model for conversation/project management
   - Constants: Provider kind ("codex"), approval policies, sandbox modes, service tiers
   - Models: `OrchestrationProject`, `OrchestrationMessage`, `OrchestrationProposedPlan`, `OrchestrationSession`, `OrchestrationCheckpointFile`, `OrchestrationCheckpointSummary`, `OrchestrationThreadActivity`, `OrchestrationLatestTurn`, `OrchestrationThread`, `OrchestrationReadModel`
   - Commands (client → server): Project CRUD, Thread CRUD, Thread runtime/interaction mode changes, Turn start/interrupt, Approval/user-input responses, Checkpoint revert, Session stop
   - Internal commands: Session set, Message deltas, Proposed plan changes, Activity append, Checkpoint created
   - WebSocket methods in `ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS`: getSnapshot, dispatchCommand, getTurnDiff, getFullThreadDiff, replayEvents
   - Support for both client and server-side command variants

7. **git.ts** (173 lines)
   - Git operation schemas:
   - Stacked actions: commit, commit_push, commit_push_pr
   - Inputs: `GitStatusInput`, `GitPullInput`, `GitRunStackedActionInput`, `GitListBranchesInput`, `GitCreateWorktreeInput`, `GitRemoveWorktreeInput`, `GitCreateBranchInput`, `GitCheckoutInput`, `GitInitInput`
   - Results: `GitStatusResult` (branch, working tree changes, upstream info, PR status), `GitListBranchesResult`, `GitCreateWorktreeResult`, `GitRunStackedActionResult`, `GitPullResult`
   - Branch schema: name, isRemote, current, isDefault, worktreePath
   - PR status: open/closed/merged with number, title, URL, base/head branches

8. **terminal.ts** (duplicate name, contains) - Already covered above

9. **keybindings.ts** (104 lines)
   - Keyboard binding configuration:
   - Static commands: terminal.toggle/split/new/close, diff.toggle, chat.new/newLocal, editor.openFavorite
   - Dynamic pattern: `script.[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,22}.run`
   - `KeybindingRule`: key (1-64 chars), command, optional when expression
   - `KeybindingShortcut`: key, metaKey, ctrlKey, shiftKey, altKey, modKey
   - AST support for when expressions: identifier, not, and, or operators (recursive)
   - Config limits: 256 max keybindings

10. **server.ts** (72 lines)
    - Server configuration and status:
    - `ServerConfig`: cwd, keybindings config path, resolved keybindings, issues array, provider statuses, available editors
    - `ServerConfigIssue`: union of keybindings.malformed-config, keybindings.invalid-entry
    - `ServerProviderStatus`: provider kind, status (ready/warning/error), available, authStatus (authenticated/unauthenticated/unknown), checked timestamp, optional message
    - `ServerUpsertKeybindingInput`, `ServerUpsertKeybindingResult`, `ServerConfigUpdatedPayload`

11. **editor.ts** (19 lines)
    - Editor definitions: Cursor, VS Code, Zed, File Manager
    - `EditorId` schema (literal union)
    - `OpenInEditorInput`: cwd, editor selection

12. **project.ts** (42 lines)
    - Project file operations:
    - `ProjectSearchEntriesInput`: cwd, query (max 256 chars), limit (1-200)
    - `ProjectEntry`: path, kind (file/directory), optional parentPath
    - `ProjectSearchEntriesResult`: entries array, truncated flag
    - `ProjectWriteFileInput`: cwd, relativePath (max 512 chars), contents
    - `ProjectWriteFileResult`: relativePath (confirms write)

13. **ws.ts** (172 lines)
    - WebSocket protocol definition:
    - Request/response envelopes with ID, result/error fields
    - Push messages for server → client events
    - Tagged union of all RPC request bodies with `_tag` discriminator
    - Method constants (37 methods): projects.*, shell.*, git.*, terminal.*, server.*
    - Push channels: terminal.event, server.welcome, server.configUpdated
    - Welcome payload: cwd, projectName, optional bootstrap IDs

14. **ipc.ts** (5,414 bytes)
    - Desktop app bridge interface:
    - `DesktopBridge`: WebSocket URL, folder picker, confirm dialog, context menu, external URL opener
    - Update system: state (disabled/idle/checking/up-to-date/available/downloading/downloaded/error), arch info, download progress
    - `NativeApi`: Grouped by domain (dialogs, terminal, projects, shell, git, contextMenu, server, orchestration)
    - Terminal methods: open, write, resize, clear, restart, close + event listeners
    - Git methods: existing branch/worktree API plus stacked action API (pull, status, runStackedAction)
    - Server methods: getConfig, upsertKeybinding
    - Orchestration methods: getSnapshot, dispatchCommand, getTurnDiff, getFullThreadDiff, replayEvents, onDomainEvent

**Test Files:**
- `provider.test.ts` - Tests ProviderSessionStartInput and ProviderSendTurnInput with Schema decoding
- `keybindings.test.ts` - Tests keybinding parsing and validation
- `orchestration.test.ts` - Tests command serialization and orchestration schema
- `terminal.test.ts` - Tests terminal input/output schemas
- `ws.test.ts` - Tests WebSocket request/response format
- `providerRuntime.test.ts` - Tests provider runtime event schemas

---

### PACKAGE INTERDEPENDENCIES

```
@t3tools/shared
  ↓ depends on
  @t3tools/contracts (workspace:*)
  effect (catalog)

@t3tools/contracts
  ↓ depends on
  effect (catalog)
```

The `shared` package uses types and constants from `contracts` to provide utilities that build upon the base type definitions.

---

### KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

1. **Schema-Driven Development:** All contracts use Effect.js `Schema` for runtime validation with compile-time type inference
2. **Branded Types:** Uses Schema's `.pipe(Schema.brand(brand))` for type-safe identifiers (ThreadId, ProjectId, etc.)
3. **Subpath Exports:** Modular imports in shared package enable tree-shaking
4. **Effect Runtime:** Net service demonstrates proper use of Effect.Layer for dependency injection
5. **Dual Module Output:** Contracts package builds to both ESM and CJS via tsdown
6. **Strict TypeScript:** Base config enforces `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, eliminating common pitfalls
7. **Monorepo Workspace:** Yarn/Pnpm workspace pattern with centralized catalog dependencies
8. **WebSocket Protocol:** Typed RPC with schema validation for all client-server communication
9. **Event-Driven Architecture:** Heavy use of discriminated unions for event types and commands

---

### ALL FILE PATHS

**Shared Package:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/packages/shared/package.json`

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed report:

## Comprehensive Monorepo Exploration Report

### Overview
This is a TypeScript monorepo (`@t3tools/monorepo`) using Bun as the package manager with Turbo for build orchestration. The repo uses Effect.js for typed functional programming, Schema for validation, and Vitest for testing.

---

### ROOT CONFIGURATION

**Root Package.json:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/package.json`

- **Workspace setup:** Uses Yarn/Pnpm workspaces pattern with `apps/*`, `packages/*`, and `scripts` directories
- **Package manager:** Bun v1.3.9 (required)
- **Node version:** ^24.13.1
- **Key scripts:** dev, build, typecheck, test, lint (oxlint), format (oxfmt)
- **Catalog dependencies:** Uses centralized version management for common libraries:
  - `effect` (from PR feed)
  - `@effect/platform-node`, `@effect/sql-sqlite-bun`, `@effect/vitest` (from PR feed)
  - `TypeScript: ^5.7.3`
  - `Vitest: ^4.0.0`
  - `tsdown: ^0.20.3`
  - `@types/bun`, `@types/node`

**Base TypeScript Config:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/tsconfig.base.json`
- Target: ES2023
- Module: ESNext
- Module resolution: Bundler (modern)
- Strict mode enabled with strict compiler checks (`noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noImplicitOverride`)
- Synth defaults allowed for compatibility

---

### PACKAGES/SHARED - UTILITIES PACKAGE

**Package:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/packages/shared/`

**Package.json:**
- Name: `@t3tools/shared`
- Version: `0.0.0-alpha.1`
- Private: true
- **Exports:** Subpath exports for modular imports:
  - `./model` → `src/model.ts`
  - `./git` → `src/git.ts`
  - `./logging` → `src/logging.ts`
  - `./shell` → `src/shell.ts`
  - `./Net` → `src/Net.ts`
- **Dependencies:** `@t3tools/contracts` (workspace), `effect` (catalog)
- **Build:** TypeScript compilation with typecheck and vitest
- **Prepare:** Effect language service patching

**TSConfig:** Extends base config, includes only `src` directory

**Source Files:**

1. **model.ts** (79 lines)
   - Re-exports model constants from `@t3tools/contracts`
   - Functions: `getModelOptions()`, `getDefaultModel()`, `normalizeModelSlug()`, `resolveModelSlug()`, `resolveModelSlugForProvider()`, `getReasoningEffortOptions()`, `getDefaultReasoningEffort()`
   - Provides model resolution logic with aliases (e.g., "5.3" → "gpt-5.3-codex")
   - Sets use set structures for model slug validation

2. **git.ts** (62 lines)
   - Utilities for git branch name handling:
   - `sanitizeBranchFragment()` - Sanitizes strings to valid git branch fragments (lowercase, max 64 chars, strips special chars)
   - `sanitizeFeatureBranchName()` - Wraps with `feature/` prefix
   - `resolveAutoFeatureBranchName()` - Generates unique feature branch names with numeric suffixes when collisions occur

3. **logging.ts** (115 lines)
   - `RotatingFileSink` class for rotating log files
   - Constructor validates `maxBytes >= 1` and `maxFiles >= 1`
   - Methods: `write()`, `rotate()`, `pruneOverflowBackups()`, `readCurrentSize()`
   - Handles file I/O with optional error throwing
   - Rotates logs when size exceeded, maintains numbered backups (.1, .2, etc)

4. **shell.ts** (39 lines)
   - Functions for shell environment handling:
   - `extractPathFromShellOutput()` - Extracts PATH from shell output using markers
   - `readPathFromLoginShell()` - Executes login shell to read PATH environment variable with 5-second timeout
   - Exports `ExecFileSyncLike` type for dependency injection

5. **Net.ts** (181 lines) - Effect-based networking service
   - `NetError` - Tagged error class
   - `NetService` - Effect service with Layer
   - Methods:
     - `canListenOnHost(port, host)` - Check if TCP server can bind
     - `isPortAvailableOnLoopback(port)` - Check both IPv4 and IPv6 availability
     - `reserveLoopbackPort(host)` - Reserve ephemeral port and release immediately
     - `findAvailablePort(preferred)` - Prefer provided port or find available one
   - Handles `EADDRNOTAVAIL` as "available" for IPv6-absent hosts
   - Uses Effect callbacks for async operations

**Test Files:**
- `model.test.ts` - Tests model slug normalization, resolution, and defaults
- `shell.test.ts` - Tests shell PATH extraction
- `Net.test.ts` - Tests port availability and reservation logic

---

### PACKAGES/CONTRACTS - SCHEMA/TYPE DEFINITIONS

**Package:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/packages/contracts/`

**Package.json:**
- Name: `@t3tools/contracts`
- Version: `0.0.8`
- Private: true
- **Exports:** 
  - `.` (main entry) - Both CJS and ESM with tsdown
  - Types: `src/index.ts`
  - Import/require patterns for dual module support
- **Files:** Distributes `dist` directory
- **Build:** `tsdown` for dual-format bundling (esm, cjs) with .d.ts
- **Dependencies:** `effect` (catalog)
- **Prepare:** Effect language service patching

**TSConfig:** 
- Extends base config
- Adds `@effect/language-service` plugin with:
  - Diagnostic severity overrides (importFromBarrel: error)
  - `@effect/platform-node` namespace import packages

**Index Export:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/packages/contracts/src/index.ts`
- Barrel exports from 11 modules: baseSchemas, ipc, terminal, provider, providerRuntime, model, ws, keybindings, server, git, orchestration, editor, project

**Source Files:**

1. **baseSchemas.ts** (45 lines)
   - Base Effect schemas for common patterns:
   - `TrimmedString`, `TrimmedNonEmptyString`
   - `NonNegativeInt`, `PositiveInt`
   - `IsoDateTime` (branded string)
   - Entity ID types (branded schemas): ThreadId, ProjectId, CommandId, EventId, MessageId, TurnId, ProviderItemId, RuntimeSessionId, RuntimeItemId, RuntimeRequestId, RuntimeTaskId, ApprovalRequestId, CheckpointRef

2. **model.ts** (58 lines)
   - Model configuration constants:
   - `CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS`: ["xhigh", "high", "medium", "low"]
   - `MODEL_OPTIONS_BY_PROVIDER.codex`: 5 models (gpt-5.4, gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.3-codex-spark, gpt-5.2-codex, gpt-5.2)
   - `DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PROVIDER.codex`: "gpt-5.4"
   - `MODEL_SLUG_ALIASES_BY_PROVIDER`: Aliases for model shortcuts
   - Schema types for `CodexModelOptions`, `ProviderModelOptions`

3. **terminal.ts** (151 lines)
   - Comprehensive terminal session/event schemas
   - Input schemas: `TerminalOpenInput`, `TerminalWriteInput`, `TerminalResizeInput`, `TerminalClearInput`, `TerminalCloseInput`, `TerminalRestartInput`
   - Constraints: cols 20-400, rows 5-200, env 128 max properties, data max 65KB
   - Session snapshot: status (starting/running/exited/error), pid, history, exit code/signal
   - Event union: Started, Output, Exited, Error, Cleared, Restarted, Activity
   - All with threadId, terminalId, timestamps

4. **provider.ts** (141 lines)
   - Provider session and turn handling schemas:
   - Session status: connecting/ready/running/error/closed
   - `ProviderSession`: provider, status, runtimeMode, model, thread/turn IDs, created/updated timestamps
   - Input schemas: `ProviderSessionStartInput`, `ProviderSendTurnInput`, `ProviderInterruptTurnInput`, `ProviderStopSessionInput`, `ProviderRespondToRequestInput`, `ProviderRespondToUserInputInput`
   - `ProviderEvent`: structured logging for sessions/notifications/requests/errors with method, message, optional turnId/itemId/requestId

5. **providerRuntime.ts** (35,676 bytes - large file)
   - Complex runtime event handling from provider perspective
   - Event sources: codex.app-server.notification/request, codex.eventmsg, codex.sdk.thread-event
   - State literals: RuntimeSessionState, RuntimeThreadState, RuntimeTurnState, RuntimePlanStepStatus, RuntimeItemStatus, RuntimeContentStreamKind, RuntimeSessionExitKind, RuntimeErrorClass
   - Canonical item types: user_message, assistant_message, reasoning, plan, command_execution, file_change, mcp_tool_call, dynamic_tool_call, collab_agent_tool_call, web_search, image_view, review_entered/exited, context_compaction, error, unknown
   - Canonical request types: command_execution_approval, file_read_approval, file_change_approval, apply_patch_approval, exec_command_approval, tool_user_input, dynamic_tool_call, auth_tokens_refresh, unknown
   - Event type unions with 48+ event types (session.*, thread.*, turn.*, item.*, content.*, request.*, task.*, hook.*, tool.*, auth.*, account.*, mcp.*, model.*, config.*, deprecation.*, files.*, runtime.*)

6. **orchestration.ts** (500+ lines)
   - Main data model for conversation/project management
   - Constants: Provider kind ("codex"), approval policies, sandbox modes, service tiers
   - Models: `OrchestrationProject`, `OrchestrationMessage`, `OrchestrationProposedPlan`, `OrchestrationSession`, `OrchestrationCheckpointFile`, `OrchestrationCheckpointSummary`, `OrchestrationThreadActivity`, `OrchestrationLatestTurn`, `OrchestrationThread`, `OrchestrationReadModel`
   - Commands (client → server): Project CRUD, Thread CRUD, Thread runtime/interaction mode changes, Turn start/interrupt, Approval/user-input responses, Checkpoint revert, Session stop
   - Internal commands: Session set, Message deltas, Proposed plan changes, Activity append, Checkpoint created
   - WebSocket methods in `ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS`: getSnapshot, dispatchCommand, getTurnDiff, getFullThreadDiff, replayEvents
   - Support for both client and server-side command variants

7. **git.ts** (173 lines)
   - Git operation schemas:
   - Stacked actions: commit, commit_push, commit_push_pr
   - Inputs: `GitStatusInput`, `GitPullInput`, `GitRunStackedActionInput`, `GitListBranchesInput`, `GitCreateWorktreeInput`, `GitRemoveWorktreeInput`, `GitCreateBranchInput`, `GitCheckoutInput`, `GitInitInput`
   - Results: `GitStatusResult` (branch, working tree changes, upstream info, PR status), `GitListBranchesResult`, `GitCreateWorktreeResult`, `GitRunStackedActionResult`, `GitPullResult`
   - Branch schema: name, isRemote, current, isDefault, worktreePath
   - PR status: open/closed/merged with number, title, URL, base/head branches

8. **terminal.ts** (duplicate name, contains) - Already covered above

9. **keybindings.ts** (104 lines)
   - Keyboard binding configuration:
   - Static commands: terminal.toggle/split/new/close, diff.toggle, chat.new/newLocal, editor.openFavorite
   - Dynamic pattern: `script.[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,22}.run`
   - `KeybindingRule`: key (1-64 chars), command, optional when expression
   - `KeybindingShortcut`: key, metaKey, ctrlKey, shiftKey, altKey, modKey
   - AST support for when expressions: identifier, not, and, or operators (recursive)
   - Config limits: 256 max keybindings

10. **server.ts** (72 lines)
    - Server configuration and status:
    - `ServerConfig`: cwd, keybindings config path, resolved keybindings, issues array, provider statuses, available editors
    - `ServerConfigIssue`: union of keybindings.malformed-config, keybindings.invalid-entry
    - `ServerProviderStatus`: provider kind, status (ready/warning/error), available, authStatus (authenticated/unauthenticated/unknown), checked timestamp, optional message
    - `ServerUpsertKeybindingInput`, `ServerUpsertKeybindingResult`, `ServerConfigUpdatedPayload`

11. **editor.ts** (19 lines)
    - Editor definitions: Cursor, VS Code, Zed, File Manager
    - `EditorId` schema (literal union)
    - `OpenInEditorInput`: cwd, editor selection

12. **project.ts** (42 lines)
    - Project file operations:
    - `ProjectSearchEntriesInput`: cwd, query (max 256 chars), limit (1-200)
    - `ProjectEntry`: path, kind (file/directory), optional parentPath
    - `ProjectSearchEntriesResult`: entries array, truncated flag
    - `ProjectWriteFileInput`: cwd, relativePath (max 512 chars), contents
    - `ProjectWriteFileResult`: relativePath (confirms write)

13. **ws.ts** (172 lines)
    - WebSocket protocol definition:
    - Request/response envelopes with ID, result/error fields
    - Push messages for server → client events
    - Tagged union of all RPC request bodies with `_tag` discriminator
    - Method constants (37 methods): projects.*, shell.*, git.*, terminal.*, server.*
    - Push channels: terminal.event, server.welcome, server.configUpdated
    - Welcome payload: cwd, projectName, optional bootstrap IDs

14. **ipc.ts** (5,414 bytes)
    - Desktop app bridge interface:
    - `DesktopBridge`: WebSocket URL, folder picker, confirm dialog, context menu, external URL opener
    - Update system: state (disabled/idle/checking/up-to-date/available/downloading/downloaded/error), arch info, download progress
    - `NativeApi`: Grouped by domain (dialogs, terminal, projects, shell, git, contextMenu, server, orchestration)
    - Terminal methods: open, write, resize, clear, restart, close + event listeners
    - Git methods: existing branch/worktree API plus stacked action API (pull, status, runStackedAction)
    - Server methods: getConfig, upsertKeybinding
    - Orchestration methods: getSnapshot, dispatchCommand, getTurnDiff, getFullThreadDiff, replayEvents, onDomainEvent

**Test Files:**
- `provider.test.ts` - Tests ProviderSessionStartInput and ProviderSendTurnInput with Schema decoding
- `keybindings.test.ts` - Tests keybinding parsing and validation
- `orchestration.test.ts` - Tests command serialization and orchestration schema
- `terminal.test.ts` - Tests terminal input/output schemas
- `ws.test.ts` - Tests WebSocket request/response format
- `providerRuntime.test.ts` - Tests provider runtime event schemas

---

### PACKAGE INTERDEPENDENCIES

```
@t3tools/shared
  ↓ depends on
  @t3tools/contracts (workspace:*)
  effect (catalog)

@t3tools/contracts
  ↓ depends on
  effect (catalog)
```

The `shared` package uses types and constants from `contracts` to provide utilities that build upon the base type definitions.

---

### KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

1. **Schema-Driven Development:** All contracts use Effect.js `Schema` for runtime validation with compile-time type inference
2. **Branded Types:** Uses Schema's `.pipe(Schema.brand(brand))` for type-safe identifiers (ThreadId, ProjectId, etc.)
3. **Subpath Exports:** Modular imports in shared package enable tree-shaking
4. **Effect Runtime:** Net service demonstrates proper use of Effect.Layer for dependency injection
5. **Dual Module Output:** Contracts package builds to both ESM and CJS via tsdown
6. **Strict TypeScript:** Base config enforces `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, eliminating common pitfalls
7. **Monorepo Workspace:** Yarn/Pnpm workspace pattern with centralized catalog dependencies
8. **WebSocket Protocol:** Typed RPC with schema validation for all client-server communication
9. **Event-Driven Architecture:** Heavy use of discriminated unions for event types and commands

---

### ALL FILE PATHS

**Shared Package:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/packages/shared/package.json`

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive report of my findings.

## Complete Web App Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Root Directories:**
- `/src` - Main source code
  - `/components` - React components
    - `/ui` - 45+ reusable UI component library (Base UI based)
  - `/hooks` - Custom React hooks (3 files)
  - `/lib` - Utility and library functions (20 files)
  - `/routes` - TanStack Router route definitions (5 files)

**Total Files:** 103 TypeScript/TSX files (excluding tests and build files)

### 2. CSS/STYLING FILES

**Location:** `/src/index.css` (single comprehensive CSS file)

**Key CSS Features:**
- **Tailwind CSS 4.0** with Vite integration
- **CSS Custom Properties (Variables)** defined at `:root` and dark variant:
  - **Color tokens:** `--color-*` (primary, secondary, destructive, info, success, warning, etc.)
  - **Spacing/Radius tokens:** `--radius`, `--radius-sm`, `--radius-md`, `--radius-lg`, `--radius-xl`, `--radius-2xl`, `--radius-3xl`, `--radius-4xl`
  - **Light mode colors:** oklch color space (e.g., `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)` for primary)
  - **Dark mode:** Separate variant with lighter oklch values and adjusted alphas

**Theme System:**
```css
:root {
  --radius: 0.625rem;
  --background: var(--color-white);
  --foreground: var(--color-neutral-800);
  --primary: oklch(0.488 0.217 264);
  /* ... 20+ theme tokens */
}

@variant dark {
  --background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-neutral-950) 95%, var(--color-white));
  --primary: oklch(0.588 0.217 264);
  /* ... */
}
```

**CSS Features Used:**
- `@import "tailwindcss"` - Tailwind integration
- `@custom-variant dark` - Custom dark mode variant
- `@theme inline` - Inline theme customization with keyframes
- `@layer base` - Base layer styling
- `color-mix()` and `--alpha()` functions for color manipulation
- CSS Grid, Flexbox, Shadow utilities
- Window drag region support (Electron)
- Custom scrollbar styling
- Chat markdown rendering styles
- Diff panel theming bridge

### 3. MAIN ENTRY POINT

**File:** `/src/main.tsx` (24 lines)

```tsx
import React from "react";
import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client";
import { RouterProvider } from "@tanstack/react-router";
import { createHashHistory, createBrowserHistory } from "@tanstack/react-router";

import "@xterm/xterm/css/xterm.css";
import "./index.css";

import { isElectron } from "./env";
import { getRouter } from "./router";
import { APP_DISPLAY_NAME } from "./branding";

const history = isElectron ? createHashHistory() : createBrowserHistory();
const router = getRouter(history);

document.title = APP_DISPLAY_NAME;

ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root") as HTMLElement).render(
  <React.StrictMode>
    <RouterProvider router={router} />
  </React.StrictMode>,
);
```

**Key Setup:**
- Uses TanStack Router for routing
- Conditional history strategy (hash for Electron, browser for web)
- React 19.0.0 with StrictMode
- Loads xterm CSS and custom index.css

### 4. EXISTING STATE MANAGEMENT, CONTEXT PROVIDERS & HOOKS

**Custom Hooks (3 files):**

1. **`useTheme()`** (`/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`)
   - Uses `useSyncExternalStore` for external state management
   - Manages light/dark/system theme preference
   - Stores theme in localStorage under key `"t3code:theme"`
   - Listens to system media query changes `(prefers-color-scheme: dark)`
   - Syncs across browser tabs via `storage` event
   - Returns: `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`

2. **`useMediaQuery()`** (`/src/hooks/useMediaQuery.ts`)
   - Simple hook for media query matching
   - Uses `useState` and `useEffect`
   - Returns boolean for media query matches

3. **`useTurnDiffSummaries()`** (`/src/hooks/useTurnDiffSummaries.ts`)
   - Related to diff rendering functionality

**State Management (Zustand):**

1. **`useTerminalStateStore`** (`/src/terminalStateStore.ts` - 533 lines)
   - Manages terminal UI state per thread
   - **Persisted via localStorage** under key `"t3code:terminal-state:v1"`
   - State shape:
     - `terminalOpen`, `terminalHeight`, `terminalIds`
     - `runningTerminalIds`, `activeTerminalId`
     - `terminalGroups`, `activeTerminalGroupId`
   - Actions: split/new/close terminals, set height, set active, manage activity
   - Extensive normalization and validation logic

2. **`useComposerDraftStore`** (`/src/composerDraftStore.ts` - 400+ lines, read 200 lines)
   - Manages composer (chat input) drafts per thread
   - **Persisted via localStorage** under key `"t3code:composer-drafts:v1"`
   - State includes:
     - `draftsByThreadId` - Per-thread drafts with prompt, images, provider/model settings
     - `draftThreadsByThreadId` - Draft thread metadata (branch, worktree path, env mode)
     - `projectDraftThreadIdByProjectId` - Project-to-draft-thread mapping
   - Handles image attachments (persisted and non-persisted)
   - Manages reasoning effort, runtime mode, interaction mode

3. **`useStore`** (`/src/store.ts`)
   - Main app state store for projects and threads
   - **Persisted via localStorage** under key `"t3code:renderer-state:v8"`
   - Stores expanded project states, threads metadata
   - Includes session status tracking, model management
   - Migrations from legacy keys (v1-v7)

**Context Providers:**

1. **`ToastProvider` & `AnchoredToastProvider`** (`/src/components/ui/toast.tsx`)
   - Toast notification system

2. **`DiffWorkerPoolProvider`** (`/src/components/ui DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx`)
   - Context provider for diff rendering worker pool
   - Uses `WorkerPoolContextProvider` from `@pierre/diffs/react`
   - Syncs diff theme based on `useTheme()` hook
   - Configurable pool size based on CPU cores (2-6 workers)

**Root Route Setup** (`/src/routes/__root.tsx`):
- Sets up `ToastProvider` and `AnchoredToastProvider`
- Loads all store hooks: `useComposerDraftStore()`, `useStore()`, `useTerminalStateStore()`
- Includes event routers and desktop project bootstrap

### 5. LOCAL STORAGE USAGE

| Key | Purpose | Version |
|-----|---------|---------|
| `t3code:theme` | Theme preference (light/dark/system) | N/A |
| `t3code:terminal-state:v1` | Terminal UI state per thread | v1 |
| `t3code:composer-drafts:v1` | Chat/composer drafts per thread | v1 |
| `t3code:renderer-state:v8` | Main app state (projects/threads) | v8 |

**Legacy keys cleaned up automatically:**
- v1-v7 of renderer-state, codething:renderer-state:v1-v4

### 6. COMPONENTS DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**UI Components Library (45+ components):**
- alert, alert-dialog, autocomplete, badge, button, card, checkbox, collapsible, combobox, command, dialog, empty, field, fieldset, form, group, input, input-group, kbd, label, menu, popover, radio-group, scroll-area, select, separator, sheet, sidebar, skeleton, spinner, switch, textarea, toast, toggle, toggle-group, tooltip

**Feature Components:**
- `BranchToolbar` / `BranchToolbarBranchSelector` - Git branch management
- `ChatView` / `ChatMarkdown` - Chat/message rendering
- `ComposerPromptEditor` - Input editor for prompts
- `DiffPanel` - Diff visualization
- `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` - Diff rendering provider
- `GitActionsControl` - Git operations UI
- `Icons` - Icon component
- `PlanSidebar` - Plan/outline sidebar
- `ProjectScriptsControl` - Project scripts runner
- `Sidebar` - Main sidebar
- `ThreadTerminalDrawer` - Terminal panel

**Component Architecture:**
- Uses Base UI React library (`@base-ui/react`) for low-level primitives
- Class Variance Authority (CVA) for variant styling

- All components styled with Tailwind CSS
- Render prop pattern support in Base UI components
- Data slot attributes for styling specificity

### 7. PACKAGE.JSON DEPENDENCIES

**Key Dependencies:**
- **React**: 19.0.0 with React DOM 19.0.0
- **Routing**: @tanstack/react-router 1.160.2
- **State Management**: zustand 5.0.11, @tanstack/react-query 5.90.0
- **UI Framework**: @base-ui/react 1.2.0

- MSW (Mock Service Worker) for API mocking

### 8. KEY INSIGHTS

**Architecture Patterns:**
1. **Zustand stores with localStorage persistence** - All major state uses Zustand with JSON serialization
2. **useSyncExternalStore for external state** - Theme management uses external store pattern
3. **React Router with TanStack** - File-based routing via `__root.tsx`, `_chat.tsx`, `_chat.$threadId.tsx`, etc.
4. **Worker pool for diffs** - CPU-intensive diff rendering offloaded to workers

- Base UI provides composable, headless components

- Data attributes for styling hooks
- Render prop pattern for flexibility

**Performance Optimizations:**
- React 19 with Babel compiler plugin

## Complete Web App Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Root Directories:**
- `/src` - Main source code
  - `/components` - React components
    - `/ui` - 45+ reusable UI component library (Base UI based)
  - `/hooks` - Custom React hooks (3 files)
  - `/lib` - Utility and library functions (20 files)
  - `/routes` - TanStack Router route definitions (5 files)

**Total Files:** 103 TypeScript/TSX files (excluding tests and build files)

### 2. CSS/STYLING FILES

**Location:** `/src/index.css` (single comprehensive CSS file)

**Key CSS Features:**
- **Tailwind CSS 4.0** with Vite integration
- **CSS Custom Properties (Variables)** defined at `:root` and dark variant:
  - **Color tokens:** `--color-*` (primary, secondary, destructive, info, success, warning, etc.)
  - **Spacing/Radius tokens:** `--radius`, `--radius-sm`, `--radius-md`, `--radius-lg`, `--radius-xl`, `--radius-2xl`, `--radius-3xl`, `--radius-4xl`
  - **Light mode colors:** oklch color space (e.g., `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)` for primary)
  - **Dark mode:** Separate variant with lighter oklch values and adjusted alphas

**Theme System:**
```css
:root {
  --radius: 0.625rem;
  --background: var(--color-white);
  --foreground: var(--color-neutral-800);
  --primary: oklch(0.488 0.217 264);
  /* ... 20+ theme tokens */
}

@variant dark {
  --background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-neutral-950) 95%, var(--color-white));
  --primary: oklch(0.588 0.217 264);
  /* ... */
}
```

**CSS Features Used:**
- `@import "tailwindcss"` - Tailwind integration
- `@custom-variant dark` - Custom dark mode variant
- `@theme inline` - Inline theme customization with keyframes
- `@layer base` - Base layer styling
- `color-mix()` and `--alpha()` functions for color manipulation
- CSS Grid, Flexbox, Shadow utilities
- Window drag region support (Electron)
- Custom scrollbar styling
- Chat markdown rendering styles
- Diff panel theming bridge

### 3. MAIN ENTRY POINT

**File:** `/src/main.tsx` (24 lines)

```tsx
import React from "react";
import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client";
import { RouterProvider } from "@tanstack/react-router";
import { createHashHistory, createBrowserHistory } from "@tanstack/react-router";

import "@xterm/xterm/css/xterm.css";
import "./index.css";

import { isElectron } from "./env";
import { getRouter } from "./router";
import { APP_DISPLAY_NAME } from "./branding";

const history = isElectron ? createHashHistory() : createBrowserHistory();
const router = getRouter(history);

document.title = APP_DISPLAY_NAME;

ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root") as HTMLElement).render(
  <React.StrictMode>
    <RouterProvider router={router} />
  </React.StrictMode>,
);
```

**Key Setup:**
- Uses TanStack Router for routing
- Conditional history strategy (hash for Electron, browser for web)
- React 19.0.0 with StrictMode
- Loads xterm CSS and custom index.css

### 4. EXISTING STATE MANAGEMENT, CONTEXT PROVIDERS & HOOKS

**Custom Hooks (3 files):**

1. **`useTheme()`** (`/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`)
   - Uses `useSyncExternalStore` for external state management
   - Manages light/dark/system theme preference
   - Stores theme in localStorage under key `"t3code:theme"`
   - Listens to system media query changes `(prefers-color-scheme: dark)`
   - Syncs across browser tabs via `storage` event
   - Returns: `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`

2. **`useMediaQuery()`** (`/src/hooks/useMediaQuery.ts`)
   - Simple hook for media query matching
   - Uses `useState` and `useEffect`
   - Returns boolean for media query matches

3. **`useTurnDiffSummaries()`** (`/src/hooks/useTurnDiffSummaries.ts`)
   - Related to diff rendering functionality

**State Management (Zustand):**

1. **`useTerminalStateStore`** (`/src/terminalStateStore.ts` - 533 lines)
   - Manages terminal UI state per thread
   - **Persisted via localStorage** under key `"t3code:terminal-state:v1"`
   - State shape:
     - `terminalOpen`, `terminalHeight`, `terminalIds`
     - `runningTerminalIds`, `activeTerminalId`
     - `terminalGroups`, `activeTerminalGroupId`
   - Actions: split/new/close terminals, set height, set active, manage activity
   - Extensive normalization and validation logic

2. **`useComposerDraftStore`** (`/src/composerDraftStore.ts` - 400+ lines, read 200 lines)
   - Manages composer (chat input) drafts per thread
   - **Persisted via localStorage** under key `"t3code:composer-drafts:v1"`
   - State includes:
     - `draftsByThreadId` - Per-thread drafts with prompt, images, provider/model settings
     - `draftThreadsByThreadId` - Draft thread metadata (branch, worktree path, env mode)
     - `projectDraftThreadIdByProjectId` - Project-to-draft-thread mapping
   - Handles image attachments (persisted and non-persisted)
   - Manages reasoning effort, runtime mode, interaction mode

3. **`useStore`** (`/src/store.ts`)
   - Main app state store for projects and threads
   - **Persisted via localStorage** under key `"t3code:renderer-state:v8"`
   - Stores expanded project states, threads metadata
   - Includes session status tracking, model management
   - Migrations from legacy keys (v1-v7)

**Context Providers:**

1. **`ToastProvider` & `AnchoredToastProvider`** (`/src/components/ui/toast.tsx`)
   - Toast notification system

2. **`DiffWorkerPoolProvider`** (`/src/components/ui DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx`)
   - Context provider for diff rendering worker pool
   - Uses `WorkerPoolContextProvider` from `@pierre/diffs/react`
   - Syncs diff theme based on `useTheme()` hook
   - Configurable pool size based on CPU cores (2-6 workers)

**Root Route Setup** (`/src/routes/__root.tsx`):
- Sets up `ToastProvider` and `AnchoredToastProvider`
- Loads all store hooks: `useComposerDraftStore()`, `useStore()`, `useTerminalStateStore()`
- Includes event routers and desktop project bootstrap

### 5. LOCAL STORAGE USAGE

| Key | Purpose | Version |
|-----|---------|---------|
| `t3code:theme` | Theme preference (light/dark/system) | N/A |
| `t3code:terminal-state:v1` | Terminal UI state per thread | v1 |
| `t3code:composer-drafts:v1` | Chat/composer drafts per thread | v1 |
| `t3code:renderer-state:v8` | Main app state (projects/threads) | v8 |

**Legacy keys cleaned up automatically:**
- v1-v7 of renderer-state, codething:renderer-state:v1-v4

### 6. COMPONENTS DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**UI Components Library (45+ components):**
- alert, alert-dialog, autocomplete, badge, button, card, checkbox, collapsible, combobox, command, dialog, empty, field, fieldset, form, group, input, input-group, kbd, label, menu, popover, radio-group, scroll-area, select, separator, sheet, sidebar, skeleton, spinner, switch, textarea, toast, toggle, toggle-group, tooltip

### 6. CONFIG FILES

**index.html:**
- Basic HTML structure

- Favicon and apple-touch-icon links
- Root div with id="root"

**vite.config.ts:**
- Plugins: TanStack Router, React (with Babel compiler), Tailwind CSS
- Dev port: 5733
- Development WebSocket HMR on localhost
- Build output to `dist/` directory
- Optional source maps based on `T3CODE_WEB_SOURCEMAP` env var

**components.json (shadcn configuration):**

```json
    "cssVariables": true
  },
  "iconLibrary": "lucide"
}
```

### 7. FONT STACK & TYPOGRAPHY (index.css - Lines 123-164)

**Body Font:**
```

```

**Monospace Font:**
```

```

### 8. CUSTOM CSS FEATURES

**Noise Effect (index.css - Lines 148-157):**
```css

  background-image: url("data:image/svg+xml,...fractalNoise...");
  opacity: 0.035;
  background-size: 256px 256px;
}
```

**Scrollbar Styling (index.css - Lines 179-227):**
- Width: 6px
- Webkit scrollbars with theme-aware colors
- Terminal scrollbars: 6px width
- Turn chip strip: scrollbar-width: none (hidden)

**Drag Region (index.css - Lines 166-177):**
- Uses webkit-app-region for frameless window support
- Electron integration for desktop app

---

### 9. COLOR FUNCTIONS & ADVANCED FEATURES

**oklch() Function:**
- Light primary: `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)` - 48.8% lightness, 0.217 chroma, 264° hue (purple)
- Dark primary: `oklch(0.588 0.217 264)` - 58.8% lightness (lighter variant)

**color-mix() Function:**
- Used throughout for dynamic color blending
- Examples: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--card) 90%, var(--background))`
- Allows semantic color mixing without hardcoding hex values

**--alpha() (CSS-in-JS Style Alpha):**
- Custom syntax: `--alpha(var(--color-black) / 4%)`
- Used for semi-transparent semantic colors
- Differences between light and dark modes

---

### 10. TAILWIND INTEGRATION

**CSS Variables Support:**
- cssVariables: true in components.json
- All semantic colors mapped to Tailwind color system
- Classes use standard Tailwind utilities: `bg-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`, `border-border`, etc.

**Custom Variant:**
```css
@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *));
```

**Keyframe Animation:**
```css
@keyframes skeleton {
  to { background-position: -200% 0; }
}
```

---

## Summary

The T3 Code web app uses a sophisticated, modern color and theme system:

1. **Theme Management**: useTheme hook with localStorage persistence and system preference detection
2. **CSS Variable Based**: All colors defined as CSS variables, bridged to Tailwind via `@theme inline`
3. **Semantic Color System**: Primary, secondary, muted, accent, destructive, info, success, warning (light/dark variants)
4. **Modern CSS Features**: oklch() for perceptual color spaces, color-mix() for dynamic blending
5. **Component Consistency**: All UI components (buttons, cards, alerts, badges) use the semantic color variables
6. **Terminal Integration**: Hardcoded xterm.js theme with light/dark variants
7. **Diff Integration**: Custom CSS override layer for seamless diff viewer theming
8. **Zero Tailwind Config File**: Config is inline in CSS, no standalone tailwind.config.ts file exists

```
variant "warning": border-warning/32, bg-warning/4
```

**Card Component (ui/card.tsx):**
```
bg-card, text-card-foreground
shadow-xs/5 with before:shadow effects for depth
dark variant: before:shadow-[0_-1px_--theme(--color-white/6%)]
```

**Chat Markdown (index.css - Lines 243-407):**
- Links: `color: var(--info-foreground)`
- Code blocks: `border: 1px solid var(--border)`, `background: var(--muted)`
- Blockquotes: `border-left: 2px solid var(--border)`, `color: var(--muted-foreground)`
- Diff surfaces: blend with `color-mix()` for seamless integration

---

### 6. CONFIG FILES

**index.html:**
- Basic HTML structure
- External Google Font: "DM Sans" (300-800 weights, italic variants)
- Favicon and apple-touch-icon links
- Root div with id="root"
- Module entry point: `/src/main.tsx`

**vite.config.ts:**
- Plugins: TanStack Router, React (with Babel compiler), Tailwind CSS
- Dev port: 5733
- Development WebSocket HMR on localhost
- Build output to `dist/` directory
- Optional source maps based on `T3CODE_WEB_SOURCEMAP` env var

**components.json (shadcn configuration):**
```json
{
  "style": "base-mira",
  "tailwind": {
    "config": "",
    "css": "src/index.css",
    "baseColor": "zinc",
    "cssVariables": true
  },
  "iconLibrary": "lucide"
}
```

---

### 7. FONT STACK & TYPOGRAPHY (index.css - Lines 123-164)

**Body Font:**
```
"DM Sans", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif
```

**Monospace Font:**
```
"SF Mono", "SFMono-Regular", Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace
```

---

### 8. CUSTOM CSS FEATURES

**Noise Effect (index.css - Lines 148-157):**
```css
body::after {
  background-image: url("data:image/svg+xml,...fractalNoise...");
  opacity: 0.035;
  background-size: 256px 256px;
}
```

**Scrollbar Styling (index.css - Lines 179-227):**
- Width: 6px
- Webkit scrollbars with theme-aware colors
- Terminal scrollbars: 6px width
- Turn chip strip: scrollbar-width: none (hidden)

**Drag Region (index.css - Lines 166-177):**
- Uses webkit-app-region for frameless window support
- Electron integration for desktop app

---

### 9. COLOR FUNCTIONS & ADVANCED FEATURES

**oklch() Function:**
- Light primary: `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)` - 48.8% lightness, 0.217 chroma, 264° hue (purple)
- Dark primary: `oklch(0.588 0.217 264)` - 58.8% lightness (lighter variant)

**color-mix() Function:**
- Used throughout for dynamic color blending
- Examples: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--card) 90%, var(--background))`
- Allows semantic color mixing without hardcoding hex values

**--alpha() (CSS-in-JS Style Alpha):**
- Custom syntax: `--alpha(var(--color-black) / 4%)`
- Used for semi-transparent semantic colors
- Differences between light and dark modes

---

### 10. TAILWIND INTEGRATION

**CSS Variables Support:**
- cssVariables: true in components.json
- All semantic colors mapped to Tailwind color system
- Classes use standard Tailwind utilities: `bg-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`, `border-border`, etc.

**Custom Variant:**
```css
@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *));
```

**Keyframe Animation:**
```css
@keyframes skeleton {
  to { background-position: -200% 0; }
}
```

---

## Summary

The T3 Code web app uses a sophisticated, modern color and theme system:

1. **Theme Management**: useTheme hook with localStorage persistence and system preference detection
2. **CSS Variable Based**: All colors defined as CSS variables, bridged to Tailwind via `@theme inline`
3. **Semantic Color System**: Primary, secondary, muted, accent, destructive, info, success, warning (light/dark variants)
4. **Modern CSS Features**: oklch() for perceptual color spaces, color-mix() for dynamic blending
5. **Component Consistency**: All UI components (buttons, cards, alerts, badges) use the semantic color variables
6. **Terminal Integration**: Hardcoded xterm.js theme with light/dark variants
7. **Diff Integration**: Custom CSS override layer for seamless diff viewer theming
8. **Zero Tailwind Config File**: Config is inline in CSS, no standalone tailwind.config.ts file exists

The best approach is keeping `:root` with light theme as the fallback (matching current behavior) while also adding `[data-theme="t3-light"]` as a more specific override. Design a concise implementation plan for a custom theme system in the T3 Code monorepo. Do NOT write any code — just produce a step-by-step plan.

## Context

T3 Code is a React/Vite web app (apps/web) backed by a Node WebSocket server, in a Bun monorepo with:
- `packages/shared` — runtime utilities, explicit subpath exports (./model, ./git, etc.), no barrel index, uses Effect.js
- `packages/contracts` — schema-only, no runtime logic
- `apps/web` — React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, TanStack Router, Zustand, Base UI components

## What needs to be built

1. **Theme type in `packages/shared`** with ThemeId (`"t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight"`), ThemeColors (background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, statusBar), Theme interface, 3 built-in theme constants, and helpers.
2. **Theme provider + useTheme hook** in web app that persists to localStorage, applies CSS custom properties, keeps `.dark` class on `<html>` for Tailwind dark: utilities.
3. **ThemeSelector component** with color swatch preview cards.
4. **CSS in index.css** defining per-theme CSS custom properties via `[data-theme="..."]` attribute selectors.

## Critical findings from codebase exploration

**Existing `useTheme.ts`** (apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts):
- Uses `useSyncExternalStore` pattern with module-level state
- Manages `"light" | "dark" | "system"` → toggles `.dark` class on `document.documentElement`
- Storage key: `"t3code:theme"`
- Module-level `applyTheme(getStored())` call prevents flash on load
- Returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme: "light"|"dark" }`

**Callers of useTheme()** and what they destructure:
- `DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx`: `const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme()` — passes to `resolveDiffThemeName(resolvedTheme)` 
- `DiffPanel.tsx`: `const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme()`
- `ChatMarkdown.tsx`: `const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme()`
- `ChatView.tsx`: `const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme()`
- `_chat.settings.tsx`: `const { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } = useTheme()` — uses `theme === option.value` comparison and `setTheme(option.value)` for the old radio button UI

**`ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx`**: uses `document.documentElement.classList.contains("dark")` — must keep `.dark` class management.

**CSS (`index.css`)** key structure:
```css
@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *));  /* Tailwind dark: utilities */
:root {
  color-scheme: light;
  --radius: 0.625rem;
  --background: ...; /* light vars */
  /* ... 19 more light vars ... */
  @variant dark {
    color-scheme: dark;
    --background: ...; /* dark vars */
    /* ... 19 dark overrides ... */
  }
}
```

**Specificity issue**: `:root.dark` (from `@variant dark` inside `:root {}`) has specificity (0,1,1). `[data-theme="t3-midnight"]` has specificity (0,1,0). So `@variant dark` wins over plain attribute selectors. Fix: use `:root[data-theme="..."]` which has specificity (0,1,1) — same as `:root.dark`, but wins by cascade order (placed later in stylesheet).

**Settings page** `_chat.settings.tsx`:
- Has `THEME_OPTIONS` = light/dark/system radio buttons rendered inline
- Uses `theme === option.value` for selection state
- This section will be replaced with the new ThemeSelector

**`packages/shared/package.json`** exports pattern:
```json
"./model": { "types": "./src/model.ts", "import": "./src/model.ts" }
```
All files are source-direct (no build step for shared).

**`__root.tsx`** wraps everything in `<ToastProvider><AnchoredToastProvider>` — ThemeProvider goes around this.

## Constraints
- Do NOT modify build configuration or add new dependencies
- `bun lint` and `bun typecheck` must pass
- `packages/contracts` is schema-only — theme data goes in `packages/shared` instead
- `packages/shared` uses Effect's language service (avoid barrel imports from effect)
- `packages/shared` tsconfig covers only `src/` — new `theme.ts` must be in `src/`

## Plan requirements

Produce a step-by-step implementation plan with:
1. Exact file paths for each new/modified file
2. What each file contains/changes (enough detail to implement without guessing)
3. How the new useTheme hook API evolves (what it returns, how old callers are affected)
4. The CSS structure for all 3 themes in index.css (specificity strategy)
5. Where ThemeSelector is placed in the UI
6. Verification steps

content.text: Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me produce the complete implementation plan. --- ## Implementation Plan: Custom Theme System for T3 Code --- ### Overview of approach The new system extends the existing `useTheme` hook rather than replacing it. The key design decisions are: - `ThemeId` becomes `"t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight"` — the three named themes, each fully self-describing (no more light/dark/system distinction at the stored level). - `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` is preserved on the hook's return value so every existing caller (`DiffWorkerPoolProvider`, `DiffPanel`, `ChatMarkdown`, `ChatView`) remains unchanged. - The `.dark` class on `` is preserved so `ThreadTerminalDrawer`'s `classList.contains("dark")` and Tailwind's `dark:` utilities continue to work. - Theme types live in `packages/shared` with a new subpath export `./theme`. No build step is needed — all shared exports are source-direct. - CSS uses `:root[data-theme="..."]` attribute selectors (specificity 0,1,1) placed after the existing `@variant dark` block (also 0,1,1), so cascade order gives them priority. --- ### Step 1 — Add theme types and constants to `packages/shared` **File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** (new file) This file has zero Effect.js imports (no Effect needed here — it is pure data). Contents: - `ThemeId` type: `"t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight"`. - `THEME_IDS` readonly array: `["t3-dark", "t3-light", "t3-midnight"]` — useful for iteration. - `ThemeColors` interface with fields: `background`, `foreground`, `accent`, `sidebar`, `editor`, `terminal`, `statusBar` — all strings (CSS color values for use in swatch previews and documentation; not consumed by CSS directly). - `Theme` interface: `{ id: ThemeId; label: string; description: string; isDark: boolean; colors: ThemeColors }`. - Three exported constants: `T3_DARK_THEME`, `T3_LIGHT_THEME`, `T3_MIDNIGHT_THEME`, each satisfying `Theme`. Example values: - `T3_DARK_THEME`: id `"t3-dark"`, label `"T3 Dark"`, isDark `true`, representative swatches (near-black bg, near-white fg, purple accent). - `T3_LIGHT_THEME`: id `"t3-light"`, label `"T3 Light"`, isDark `false`, white bg, neutral-800 fg, purple accent. - `T3_MIDNIGHT_THEME`: id `"t3-midnight"`, label `"T3 Midnight"`, isDark `true`, deeper near-black bg (#0a0a12 equivalent), blue-shifted accent. - `BUILT_IN_THEMES: readonly Theme[]` = `[T3_DARK_THEME, T3_LIGHT_THEME, T3_MIDNIGHT_THEME]`. - Helper `isThemeId(value: unknown): value is ThemeId` — checks string membership in `THEME_IDS`. - Helper `getThemeById(id: ThemeId): Theme` — returns the matching constant. - Default export: none (explicit named exports only, matching codebase style). **File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/packages/shared/package.json`** (modify) Add a new entry inside `"exports"`: ```json "./theme": { "types": "./src/theme.ts", "import": "./src/theme.ts" } ``` No other changes to `package.json`. --- ### Step 2 — Replace `useTheme.ts` in the web app **File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** (rewrite in-place) The module-level store pattern (`useSyncExternalStore` + module-level listeners + `emitChange`) is kept because it works well and prevents flash on load. **Storage key**: keep `"t3code:theme"` — but stored values change from `"light" | "dark" | "system"` to `ThemeId`. Old stored values (`"light"`, `"dark"`, `"system"`) must be migrated on first read. **Internal logic changes**: - Import `ThemeId`, `isThemeId` from `@t3tools/shared/theme`. - `getStored()` reads `localStorage`, calls `isThemeId(raw)`. If not valid, applies migration: `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"system"` and anything else → `"t3-dark"` (safe default). Writes migrated value back to localStorage immediately. - `applyTheme(id: ThemeId)`: 1. Sets `document.documentElement.setAttribute("data-theme", id)`. 2. Resolves `isDark` by importing the `getThemeById` helper and checking `isDark` field. 3. Calls `document.documentElement.classList.toggle("dark", isDark)`. 4. The `suppressTransitions` logic (`.no-transitions` class + `requestAnimationFrame` removal) is preserved unchanged. - The system preference media query and cross-tab `storage` event listeners remain, but `"system"` is no longer a valid `ThemeId`. Remove system-preference logic (the system option is being replaced by explicit named themes). The media query listener can be dropped entirely. - `getSnapshot()` returns `{ themeId: ThemeId }`. No `systemDark` needed. **Return value of `useTheme()`** — this is the API compatibility contract: ```typescript { theme: ThemeId, // was Theme ("light"|"dark"|"system") — callers that read this setTheme: (id: ThemeId) => void, resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark", // preserved — derived from getThemeById(themeId).isDark } ``` `resolvedTheme` is derived as: `getThemeById(themeId).isDark ? "dark" : "light"`. This keeps `DiffWorkerPoolProvider`, `DiffPanel`, `ChatMarkdown`, and `ChatView` unchanged — they all only destructure `resolvedTheme`. The `_chat.settings.tsx` caller destructures `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }` and compares `theme === option.value`. After this step that comparison will no longer work (old `THEME_OPTIONS` values are `"system" | "light" | "dark"`, new `theme` is `ThemeId`). That's intentional — step 5 replaces that UI section entirely. Module-level `applyTheme(getStored())` call is preserved, ensuring no flash before React hydrates. --- ### Step 3 — Add per-theme CSS custom properties in `index.css` **File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/apps/web/src/index.css`** (append new rules after the existing `:root { ... }` block, before `body { ... }`) **Specificity strategy**: Use `:root[data-theme="..."]` selectors. These have specificity (0,1,1) — same as `:root.dark` produced by the `@variant dark` inside `:root {}`. They win by cascade order because they appear later in the stylesheet. The three new blocks share the same CSS variable names as the existing `:root` declarations. They override only what differs from the base `:root` (light) values: **`:root[data-theme="t3-light"]`** — mirrors the existing `:root` light values exactly. Included for completeness so explicitly selecting "T3 Light" is stable even if the `:root` defaults drift. Sets `color-scheme: light`. **`:root[data-theme="t3-dark"]`** — mirrors the existing `@variant dark` block values exactly. Sets `color-scheme: dark` plus the same dark variable values. This replaces what was previously the `.dark` class behavior for these CSS vars. **`:root[data-theme="t3-midnight"]`** — new dark variant with deeper background. Representative differences: - `color-scheme: dark` - `--background`: a deeper near-black, e.g. `oklch(0.07 0.02 264)` (bluer-black than neutral-950) - `--foreground`: slightly cooler white, e.g. `var(--color-neutral-50)` with blue tint - `--primary`: same purple or shifted slightly bluer, e.g. `oklch(0.62 0.22 270)` - `--card`: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 96%, var(--color-white))` - All other dark-mode vars (`--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, semantic colors) same as `t3-dark` dark values This means `t3-midnight` shares most vars with `t3-dark` but has a noticeably deeper background and card surface. Note: the existing `@variant dark` block inside `:root {}` is **not removed**. It continues to handle the `.dark` class used by Tailwind `dark:` utility classes. The new `:root[data-theme="..."]` blocks override the CSS custom property values for the theme palette; the `.dark` class continues to control which Tailwind variants apply. Both mechanisms are needed simultaneously. --- ### Step 4 — Create `ThemeSelector` component **File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775494207507-s9bh7q/apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** (new file) Imports: - `BUILT_IN_THEMES` from `@t3tools/shared/theme` - `useTheme` from `../hooks/useTheme` Structure: a single exported function component `ThemeSelector`. Internally calls `const { theme, setTheme } = useTheme()`. Renders a `div` with `role="radiogroup"` and `aria-label="Theme"`. Maps over `BUILT_IN_THEMES` and renders a card per theme. Each card: - `

tool_use_result.prompt:
Design a concise implementation plan for a custom theme system in the T3 Code monorepo. Do NOT write any code — just produce a step-by-step plan.

## Context

T3 Code is a React/Vite web app (apps/web) backed by a Node WebSocket server, in a Bun monorepo with:
- `packages/shared` — runtime utilities, explicit subpath exports (./model, ./git, etc.), no barrel index, uses Effect.js
- `packages/contracts` — schema-only, no runtime logic
- `apps/web` — React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, TanStack Router, Zustand, Base UI components

## What needs to be built

1. **Theme type in `packages/shared`** with ThemeId (`"t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight"`), ThemeColors (background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, statusBar), Theme interface, 3 built-in theme constants, and helpers.
2. **Theme provider + useTheme hook** in web app that persists to localStorage, applies CSS custom properties, keeps `.dark` class on `<html>` for Tailwind dark: utilities.
3. **ThemeSelector component** with color swatch preview cards.
4. **CSS in index.css** defining per-theme CSS custom properties via `[data-theme="..."]` attribute selectors.

## Critical findings from codebase exploration

**Existing `useTheme.ts`** (apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts):
- Uses `useSyncExternalStore` pattern with module-level state
- Manages `"light" | "dark" | "system"` → toggles `.dark` class on `document.documentElement`
- Storage key: `"t3code:theme"`
- Module-level `applyTheme(getStored())` call prevents flash on load
- Returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme: "light"|"dark" }`

**Callers of useTheme()** and what they destructure:
- `DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx`: `const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme()` — passes to `resolveDiffThemeName(resolvedTheme)` 
- `DiffPanel.tsx`: `const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme()`
- `ChatMarkdown.tsx`: `const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme()`
- `ChatView.tsx`: `const { resolvedTheme } = useTheme()`
- `_chat.settings.tsx`: `const { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } = useTheme()` — uses `theme === option.value` comparison and `setTheme(option.value)` for the old radio button UI

**`ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx`**: uses `document.documentElement.classList.contains("dark")` — must keep `.dark` class management.

**CSS (`index.css`)** key structure:
```css
@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *));  /* Tailwind dark: utilities */
:root {
  color-scheme: light;
  --radius: 0.625rem;
  --background: ...; /* light vars */
  /* ... 19 more light vars ... */
  @variant dark {
    color-scheme: dark;
    --background: ...; /* dark vars */
    /* ... 19 dark overrides ... */
  }
}
```

**Specificity issue**: `:root.dark` (from `@variant dark` inside `:root {}`) has specificity (0,1,1). `[data-theme="t3-midnight"]` has specificity (0,1,0). So `@variant dark` wins over plain attribute selectors. Fix: use `:root[data-theme="..."]` which has specificity (0,1,1) — same as `:root.dark`, but wins by cascade order (placed later in stylesheet).

**Settings page** `_chat.settings.tsx`:
- Has `THEME_OPTIONS` = light/dark/system radio buttons rendered inline
- Uses `theme === option.value` for selection state
- This section will be replaced with the new ThemeSelector

**`packages/shared/package.json`** exports pattern:
```json
"./model": { "types": "./src/model.ts", "import": "./src/model.ts" }
```
All files are source-direct (no build step for shared).

**`__root.tsx`** wraps everything in `<ToastProvider><AnchoredToastProvider>` — ThemeProvider goes around this.

## Constraints
- Do NOT modify build configuration or add new dependencies
- `bun lint` and `bun typecheck` must pass
- `packages/contracts` is schema-only — theme data goes in `packages/shared` instead
- `packages/shared` uses Effect's language service (avoid barrel imports from effect)
- `packages/shared` tsconfig covers only `src/` — new `theme.ts` must be in `src/`

## Plan requirements

Produce a step-by-step implementation plan with:
1. Exact file paths for each new/modified file
2. What each file contains/changes (enough detail to implement without guessing)
3. How the new useTheme hook API evolves (what it returns, how old callers are affected)
4. The CSS structure for all 3 themes in index.css (specificity strategy)
5. Where ThemeSelector is placed in the UI
6. Verification steps

input.summary:
Here's the full implementation plan for the custom theme system:

## What's being built

A named-theme system replacing the old light/dark/system toggle with three full-palette themes: **T3 Dark**, **T3 Light**, and **T3 Midnight**.

## 8 files touched

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` | **New** — `ThemeId`, `ThemeColors`, `Theme` types + 3 built-in theme constants, `BUILT_IN_THEMES`, `isThemeId()`, `getThemeById()` |
| `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `"./theme"` subpath export |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | **Rewrite** — same `useSyncExternalStore` pattern, new `ThemeId` storage, migration from old `"light"`/`"dark"`/`"system"` values, `resolvedTheme: "light"\|"dark"` preserved |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` | **New** — thin wrapper that calls `useTheme()` for lifecycle sync, wraps root |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **New** — color swatch preview cards using `BUILT_IN_THEMES`, calls `setTheme()` |
| `apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx` | Add `<ThemeProvider>` around the root view |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Replace old radio-button appearance section with `<ThemeSelector />` |
| `apps/web/src/index.css` | Append `:root[data-theme="t3-light"]`, `:root[data-theme="t3-dark"]`, `:root[data-theme="t3-midnight"]` blocks + add 4 new surface tokens (`--theme-sidebar`, `--theme-editor`, `--theme-terminal`, `--theme-status-bar`) |

## Key design decisions

- **No new dependencies** — pure TypeScript data in `packages/shared`, no Effect.js needed
- **CSS specificity**: `:root[data-theme="..."]` = (0,1,1), same as the existing `:root.dark` from `@variant dark`. Placed *after* the existing block, so cascade order wins. The existing `@variant dark` block is kept intact for Tailwind `dark:` utility classes.
- **`.dark` class** is still toggled by `applyTheme()` so `ThreadTerminalDrawer`, scrollbar styles, and all Tailwind `dark:` utilities work unchanged
- **`resolvedTheme: "light"|"dark"`** is preserved on the hook return, so `DiffWorkerPoolProvider`, `DiffPanel`, `ChatMarkdown`, and `ChatView` need zero changes
- **Migration**: old stored values (`"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"system"` → `"t3-dark"`) handled in `getStored()`

## Theme System Plan — 8 Files

### New files (3)
- **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — Pure TypeScript types (`ThemeId`, `ThemeColors`, `Theme`) and the 3 built-in theme constants with their swatch colors. Zero Effect.js dependencies.
- **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — Thin wrapper component that subscribes to the theme hook for React lifecycle sync; added to `__root.tsx`.
